mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict direction in backtests with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal spending and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative 20-day return in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which expands aggregate supply capacity and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in fewer than 55% of cases during recovery phases, or the 60-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.15 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates a resilient labor market that sustains consumer purchasing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the CPI spike, or the signal fails to predict direction in backtests with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly real GDP growth and monthly real personal spending where spending outpaces GDP indicates robust consumer demand driving corporate earnings, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative 60-day return in > 60% of occurrences, or the joint effect loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate during a high-inflation environment compresses equity discount rates and triggers portfolio de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in fewer than 55% of cases during high-inflation periods, or the 20-day forward return correlation with rate hikes falls below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.
